steps.title: Map regions to neighborhoods
steps.content: Mandarin is widely spoken in north China and is dominant in Flushing, Queens. Cantonese and Fuzhounese are widely spoken in south China and are dominant in Chinatown, Manhattan.

steps.title: Reject unsupported inferences
steps.content: A adds a claim about immigration timing and multilingual ability. C adds unsupported population totals. D adds unsupported claims about moving between boroughs. The regional language pattern supports the inference that immigrants from north and south China tend to settle in different boroughs. The answer is B.
